text: HRV: 38 ms (Low, below 30-day baseline of 52 ms)
rationale: Device-flagged low against the user's own baseline; sheet marks HRV as High relevance with a 'Low' primary trigger tied to stress/lower recovery.

text: HRV is running below this user's own baseline, which the sheet ties to higher stress or lower recovery. Only same-device, own-baseline trends are meaningful here — there is no universal reference range for HRV.
rationale: Synthesized from the Internal Notes column for HRV, which flags it High relevance, baseline-relative, and device-method-dependent.

text: HRV reflects small natural variations in the time between your heartbeats, used as a general signal of recovery and stress balance. What's normal is very personal, so your own trend is what counts, and yours is currently below where it usually sits.
rationale: Patient-friendly synthesis drawn from the Explanation column for HRV.
referencesource: BloomCell_Wearable_markers.xlsx - Wearable Markers sheet - Row 5, Marker: HRV

text: HRV: 38 ms (Low, below 30-day baseline of 52 ms)
Focus on recovery with 5-10 minutes of breathing daily and add yoga 2-3 times per week. Since HRV is low, add an easier deload week and try to limit high-intensity training while already fatigued.
rationale: Taken directly from the 30-Day Plan column for HRV.
referencesource: BloomCell_Wearable_markers.xlsx - Wearable Markers sheet - Row 5, Marker: HRV

text: HRV: 38 ms (Low, below 30-day baseline of 52 ms)
rationale: Flagged value driving the long-term recommendation.

text: Follow your own multi-day HRV trend rather than single readings, which are naturally noisy. Persistent low readings alongside feeling run-down are worth discussing with your healthcare provider.
rationale: Taken directly from the Long-Term Plan column for HRV.
